details.text: Derivative: تُقَدِّمُوا from قَدَّمَ and it here it holds the meaning of تَتَقَدَّمَ which is to place yourselves ahead, before or to give yourselves priority over something or someone. In this context, when Allah and His Messenger ﷺ say something or decide a matter in terms of a command or prohibition, you should put anything that you may want or desire before that.

Derivative: إِخْوَةٌ (Ikhwah - Brothers) is the plural of أَخٌ which means a brother. It indicates to blood relation and implies a closer relationship than the word إِخْوان (Ikhwaan - Brothers) which implies a more general brotherhood.
Derivative: يَسْخَرْ means to mock or ridicule. It means putting someone down in respect to any matter.
Derivative: تَلْمِزُوا from لَمَزَ means to find faults and flaws in someone and bring them out whether they are true or not which is used to bring that person down.

Derivative: تَنَابَزُوا – نَبَزَ – نَبْزٌ means to give someone an insulting or derisive name and تَنَابَزُوا means to give each other insulting names. In this context, it means to not call each other by insulting or offensive names.
Derivative: الظَّنِّ from ظَنَّ means to make an assumption. ظَنٌّ is an assumption and here it relates to assuming the intent of another person.

Derivative: يَغْتَبْ from اِغْتابَ which means to backbite. غِيبَة (Gheebah - Backbiting) does not mean telling or spreading a lie about someone whilst they are not there. That is a نَمِيمَة (Nameemah – Slander or defamation). Rather غِيبَة (Gheebah - Backbiting) is sharing or spreading private or secret matters related to a person even if they are true.
Derivative: يَغُضُّونَ from غَضَّ and it means to lower or hold back and restrain. Here it is in the context of one’s voice.
Derivative: تَبَيَّنُوا from تَبَيَّنَ. بَانَ – يَبِينُ means to become clear. تَبَيَّنَ means to verify something by scrutinising and distinguishing every part of a matter.
Derivative: اقْتَتَلُوا from اِقْتَتَلَ – اِقْتِتَال to fight and kill one another.
Derivative: تَفِيءَ from فَاءَ which means to return to what is good and right.
Derivative: الْعَدْلِ means justice. قِسْط (Qist) is another word for justice however عَدْل (‘Adl) refers to open and transparent justice.

Derivative: أَقْسِطُوا from أَقْسَطَ which means to deal with قِسْط (Qist) and it refers to public and private justice or fairness. It means to act fairly and applies to all affairs of life in contrast to عَدْل (‘Adl) which is more applicable to the scenario of courts and passing judgements.
